paper_title: Fracttalix Sentinel v12: A Three-Channel Streaming Anomaly Detector for FRM Regime Identification
inference_rule_inventory: IR-1 through IR-8 — Fracttalix Phase 1-2 Merged, S42

phase_ready.note: I-9 all 7 steps SATISFIED. One live placeholder: PH-SFW.1 — P1 FRM physics dependency (regime/Hopf channel). P1 is PHASE-READY; PH-SFW.1 does not block. 374 tests passing on main at commit 105c775.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: FRM governing law
claim_registry.statement: Sentinel v12 implements the Fractal Rhythm Model f(t) = B + A·e^(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ) as its theoretical foundation. Parameters ω = π/(2·τ_gen) and λ ≈ |α|/(Γ·τ_gen) are derived from Hopf bifurcation physics (P1 Sections 3–4, Session 43). Zero free parameters.
claim_registry.source: fracttalix/__init__.py import chain; fracttalix/steps/frequency.py; fracttalix/steps/foundation.py RegimeStep

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Package version
claim_registry.statement: This AI layer describes Fracttalix Sentinel v12.0.0 as defined by __version__ = '12.0.0' in fracttalix/__init__.py (lines 4–11, with importlib.metadata fallback).

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Maintenance burden μ — architectural honesty feature
claim_registry.statement: μ = 1 − κ̄ where κ̄ is the mean cross-frequency coupling score (composite_coupling_score from CrossFrequencyCouplingStep). This is an engineering heuristic surfaced in Sentinel output to signal epistemic incompleteness: low coupling implies high coordination overhead and therefore high inferred maintenance burden. μ is NOT derived from Tainter's socioeconomic model, FRM physics, or any energy-fraction derivation. The algebraic decomposition in the implementation (coupling_cost = κ̄(1−κ̄), productive_surplus = κ̄²) is bookkeeping only. μ ∈ [0,1], dimensionless and window-size-independent. Regime thresholds (empirically set, not calibrated): TAINTER_CRITICAL (μ ≥ 0.9), TAINTER_WARNING (μ ≥ 0.75), REDUCED_RESERVE (μ ≥ 0.5), HEALTHY (μ < 0.5).

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:D]
claim_registry.name: Phi-kappa separation metric
claim_registry.statement: phi_kappa_separation = Φ − κ̄, where Φ is the Kuramoto order parameter (KuramotoOrderStep, Step 34) and κ̄ is mean coupling strength (ThroughputEstimationStep, Step 26). Interpretation mapping: separation > 0.05 → COHERENCE_LED (coherence exceeds coupling — possible intervention signature); separation < −0.05 → COUPLING_LED (coupling exceeds coherence — organic degradation); |separation| ≤ 0.05 → BALANCED. This is a definitional signal classification, not a causal claim about intervention.

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Three-channel detection completeness
claim_registry.statement: The three-channel pipeline (statistical Channel 1 via z-score/CUSUM, oscillatory Channel 2 via FRM-derived frequency decomposition, regime Channel 3 via Hopf-grounded regime detection) jointly detects all alert conditions that any single-channel sub-pipeline would detect. No alert type is exclusive to a channel subset in a way that the combined 37-step pipeline misses.
claim_registry.source: fracttalix/steps/__init__.py _build_default_pipeline lines 58–108; fracttalix/types.py AlertType enum lines 119–133

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS alert_class A IN alert_type_universe SUCH THAT single_channel_detectable(A) = TRUE AND combined_pipeline_detectable(A) = FALSE

WHERE.alert_type_universe: set · all AlertType enum values · fracttalix/types.py lines 119–133 · {POINT_ANOMALY, CONTEXTUAL_ANOMALY, COLLECTIVE_ANOMALY, DRIFT_ANOMALY, VARIANCE_ANOMALY, REGIME_CHANGE, EWS_WARNING, BAND_ANOMALY, COUPLING_DEGRADATION, STRUCTURAL_RHYTHMIC_DECOUPLING, CASCADE_PRECURSOR}
WHERE.single_channel_detectable: binary · TRUE if alert_class A is fired by at least one of {statistical_channel_steps, oscillatory_channel_steps, regime_channel_steps} operating on a synthetic test stream
WHERE.combined_pipeline_detectable: binary · TRUE if alert_class A is fired by the full 37-step _build_default_pipeline on the same synthetic test stream
WHERE.statistical_channel_steps: set · {CoreEWMAStep, CUSUMStep, VarCUSUMStep, PageHinkleyStep} · Steps 1,4,6,7 · fracttalix/steps/foundation.py
WHERE.oscillatory_channel_steps: set · {FrequencyDecompositionStep, RPIStep, RFIStep, SSIStep, PEStep, BandAnomalyStep, CrossFrequencyCouplingStep} · Steps 3,12,13,14,15,21,22

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each AlertType in alert_type_universe, verify it is reachable via at least one step in combined_pipeline_detectable; enumerate alert_type → step mapping in test_steps_*.py; finite enumeration over 11 AlertType values
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: combined_pipeline_detectable = FALSE for any A where single_channel_detectable = TRUE → FALSIFIED; all 11 alert types reachable in combined pipeline → NOT FALSIFIED
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Combined pipeline includes all 37 steps which are a superset of each channel's steps. By construction, any alert fired by a channel sub-pipeline is also firable by the full pipeline because all steps are present. Threshold: zero missed alert types — any gap is a regression.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {AlertType.BAND_ANOMALY fired by oscillatory_channel_steps on a signal with isolated band power spike, but combined_pipeline_detectable=FALSE} → FALSIFIED. Assessment: BandAnomalyStep (Step 21) is included in _build_default_pipeline → combined_pipeline_detectable=TRUE → NOT FALSIFIED → PASS

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Test suite coverage of detection pipeline
claim_registry.statement: The 374-test suite (v12.0.0, 11 test modules) provides full code coverage of all detection code paths in the three-channel pipeline (fracttalix/steps/*.py).
claim_registry.source: tests/ directory (11 test files: test_steps_foundation, test_steps_temporal, test_steps_frequency, test_steps_complexity, test_steps_channels, test_steps_physics, test_detector, test_config, test_backward_compat, test_benchmark, test_multistream); fracttalix/steps/*.py

WHERE.uncovered_lines_count: integer · count of executable source lines in detection_steps_modules with zero test coverage · as reported by coverage.py statement coverage
WHERE.detection_steps_modules: set · {fracttalix/steps/foundation.py, fracttalix/steps/temporal.py, fracttalix/steps/frequency.py, fracttalix/steps/complexity.py, fracttalix/steps/channels.py, fracttalix/steps/physics.py}

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Run: pip install coverage; coverage run -m pytest tests/; coverage report --include='fracttalix/steps/*.py' --show-missing; read Miss column; uncovered_lines_count = sum of Miss values; finite and deterministic
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: uncovered_lines_count = 0 → NOT FALSIFIED; uncovered_lines_count ≥ 1 → FALSIFIED (strict: any missed line is a coverage gap)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: 374 tests across 11 modules as of v12.0.0. Work order originally cited 115 tests — that was an earlier version count; 374 is the count at commit 105c775 on main (2026-03-10). Coverage tool: Python coverage.py. 100% statement coverage of detection steps is claimed. Note: optional-dependency branches (numpy=False, scipy=False) may reduce to 99%+ in standard environments where numpy is absent.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {coverage report shows 5 missed lines in fracttalix/steps/physics.py DiagnosticWindowStep} → uncovered_lines_count = 5 > 0 → FALSIFIED. Assessment: DiagnosticWindowStep is exercised in test_steps_physics.py → expected 0 missed lines → NOT FALSIFIED (if confirmed)

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Backward compatibility — v7.x kwargs
claim_registry.statement: All v7.x kwargs accepted by SentinelDetector() in v12 are correctly mapped to SentinelConfig equivalents via _legacy_kwargs_to_config. Passing any listed legacy kwarg raises no exception and produces the correct SentinelConfig field value.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS kwarg_key K IN legacy_mapping_keys SUCH THAT (raises_exception(K) = TRUE OR config_value_matches(K) = FALSE)

WHERE.legacy_mapping_keys: set · 35 keys: 34 direct entries in _legacy_kwargs_to_config mapping dict (fracttalix/detector.py lines 30–65) plus rsi_window alias (line 71–72) · keys: alpha, dev_alpha, multiplier, warmup_periods, regime_threshold, regime_alpha_boost, multivariate, n_channels, cov_alpha, rpi_window, rfi_window, rpi_threshold, rfi_threshold, pe_order, pe_window, pe_threshold, ews_window, ews_threshold, sti_window, tps_window, osc_damp_window, osc_threshold, cpd_window, cpd_threshold, ph_delta, ph_lambda, var_cusum_k, var_cusum_h, seasonal_period, quantile_threshold_mode, aqb_window, aqb_q_low, aqb_q_high, history_maxlen, csv_path, rsi_window
WHERE.raises_exception: binary · TRUE if SentinelDetector(**{K: valid_test_value_for_K}) raises any Python exception
WHERE.config_value_matches: binary · TRUE if SentinelDetector(**{K: v}).config.<mapped_field> equals v for a valid test value v
WHERE.valid_test_value_for_K: scalar · a valid value for kwarg K consistent with SentinelConfig field type and constraint (e.g. alpha=0.2, multiplier=3.0)

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each key K in legacy_mapping_keys, construct SentinelDetector(**{K: valid_test_value_for_K}); verify no exception raised; read config.<mapped_field> and verify equals valid_test_value_for_K; finite enumeration over 35 keys
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: any exception raised for any key → FALSIFIED; any config_value_matches = FALSE → FALSIFIED (zero tolerance for compat regression)
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: 34 direct v7.x kwargs map 1:1 to SentinelConfig fields of identical name. rsi_window is a v7.x alias for rpi_window (lines 71–72). All 35 keys exercised in tests/test_backward_compat.py. Detector_7_10 is an alias for SentinelDetector (line 412) confirming backward-compat interface.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {SentinelDetector(alpha=0.2) raises TypeError: unexpected keyword argument 'alpha'} → raises_exception(alpha)=TRUE → FALSIFIED. Assessment: _legacy_kwargs_to_config maps 'alpha' directly to SentinelConfig(alpha=0.2) → no exception expected → NOT FALSIFIED → PASS

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: Cascade precursor conjunction requirement
claim_registry.statement: The CASCADE_PRECURSOR alert (CRITICAL severity) fires if and only if all three conditions are simultaneously true: (1) COUPLING_DEGRADATION alert active, (2) STRUCTURAL_RHYTHMIC_DECOUPLING alert active, (3) at least cascade_ews_threshold (default 2) EWS indicators elevated. Any single or pair of conditions alone cannot trigger it.
claim_registry.source: fracttalix/steps/channels.py CascadePrecursorStep.update() lines 379–422; fracttalix/config.py cascade_ews_threshold line 202

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: cascade_precursor_fires = TRUE AND (coupling_active = FALSE OR decoupling_active = FALSE OR elevated_ews_count < cascade_ews_threshold_val)

WHERE.cascade_precursor_fires: binary · ctx.scratch['cascade_precursor_active'] after CascadePrecursorStep.update(ctx) · fracttalix/steps/channels.py line 421
WHERE.coupling_active: binary · ctx.scratch['coupling_degradation_active'] as read by CascadePrecursorStep line 385
WHERE.decoupling_active: binary · ctx.scratch['sr_decoupling_active'] as read by CascadePrecursorStep line 386
WHERE.elevated_ews_count: integer · count of values in {ews_score, anomaly_score, rfi} that individually exceed config.ews_threshold · CascadePrecursorStep lines 388–395

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Construct test StepContext instances for all 8 Boolean combinations of (coupling_active, decoupling_active, elevated_ews≥2); verify cascade_precursor_fires = TRUE only when all three conditions satisfied; finite 8-case enumeration
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: cascade_precursor_fires = TRUE when any condition is FALSE → FALSIFIED; cascade_precursor_fires = FALSE when all three conditions TRUE (and not in warmup) → also FALSIFIED
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Conjunction is an explicit safety design gate: CASCADE_PRECURSOR carries AlertSeverity.CRITICAL and recommends immediate review (see alert message). Single-channel anomaly must not escalate to CRITICAL alone. cascade_ews_threshold=2 means 2 of 3 EWS indicators {ews_score, anomaly_score, rfi} must exceed ews_threshold (default 0.6). All three conditions verified in test_steps_channels.py.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {coupling_active=TRUE, decoupling_active=FALSE, elevated_ews_count=3, cascade_precursor_fires=TRUE} → decoupling_active=FALSE but fires=TRUE → FALSIFIED. Assessment: CascadePrecursorStep line 398 requires 'coupling_active and decoupling_active' → both must be True → NOT FALSIFIED → PASS

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: SentinelConfig parameter validation
claim_registry.statement: SentinelConfig raises ValueError in __post_init__ for any out-of-range parameter: alpha∉(0,1], dev_alpha∉(0,1], multiplier≤0, warmup_periods<1, n_channels<1, or aqb_q_low≥aqb_q_high. Valid parameters never raise.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: EXISTS invalid_params P IN invalid_param_universe SUCH THAT raises_ValueError(P) = FALSE

WHERE.invalid_param_universe: set · parameter dicts violating any of: alpha∉(0,1], dev_alpha∉(0,1], multiplier≤0, warmup_periods<1, n_channels<1, aqb_q_low≥aqb_q_high · fracttalix/config.py __post_init__ lines 222–233
WHERE.raises_ValueError: binary · TRUE if SentinelConfig(**P) raises Python ValueError

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each of the 6 validation rules, construct one representative invalid parameter dict P; call SentinelConfig(**P); verify ValueError is raised; 6 test cases minimum; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: no ValueError for any invalid_param_set → FALSIFIED (zero tolerance for silent invalid config); ValueError for a valid param set → also FALSIFIED
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Validation rules: (1) 0 < alpha ≤ 1.0, (2) 0 < dev_alpha ≤ 1.0, (3) multiplier > 0, (4) warmup_periods ≥ 1, (5) n_channels ≥ 1, (6) 0 < aqb_q_low < aqb_q_high < 1. SentinelConfig is a frozen dataclass (slots=True) — validation runs at __init__ time via __post_init__. Tested in tests/test_config.py.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {SentinelConfig(alpha=0.0) raises no exception} → alpha=0.0 violates 0 < alpha ≤ 1 but raises_ValueError=FALSE → FALSIFIED. Assessment: __post_init__ line 222 checks 'not (0.0 < alpha ≤ 1.0)' → appends error → raises ValueError → NOT FALSIFIED → PASS

claim_registry.label: [TYPE:F]
claim_registry.name: State persistence round-trip
claim_registry.statement: save_state() serializes the full detector state to JSON and load_state() restores it correctly: the step counter _n is preserved exactly, and the per-step state_dict() for all 37 pipeline steps is restored such that subsequent detection results are reproducible.
claim_registry.source: fracttalix/detector.py save_state() lines 259–279; load_state() lines 281–290; tests/test_detector.py

WHERE.pre_save_step: integer · detector._n after T=50 warmup observations, before save_state()
WHERE.post_load_result_keys: set · keys present in fresh_detector.update_and_check(test_value) after load
WHERE.pre_save_result_keys: set · keys present in detector.update_and_check(same_test_value) before save
WHERE.test_value: float · identical scalar observation used for pre- and post-load update

falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Run detector for T=50 steps; call save_state(); create fresh SentinelDetector(same_config); load_state(save_json_str); compare _n and result key sets; finite
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: post_load_step ≠ pre_save_step → FALSIFIED; result key mismatch → FALSIFIED
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: save_state includes 'n': self._n, 'config': dataclasses.asdict, 'bank': bank.state_dict(), 'steps': list of per-step state_dict(). load_state restores _n (line 284) and per-step state (lines 286–290). JSON round-trip via json.dumps/loads. State persistence exercised in tests/test_detector.py.

claim_registry.sample_falsification_observation: O = {fresh_detector._n = 0 after load_state(save of detector at _n=50)} → post_load_step=0 ≠ pre_save_step=50 → FALSIFIED. Assessment: save_state includes 'n': self._n (line 268) and load_state restores via self._n = sd.get('n', 0) (line 284) → post_load_step=50 → NOT FALSIFIED → PASS

placeholder_register.description: Sentinel v12 oscillatory channel (FrequencyDecompositionStep, RPIStep, RFIStep, SSIStep, PhaseExtractionStep, PACCoefficientStep, KuramotoOrderStep) and regime channel (RegimeStep) are grounded in the FRM Hopf bifurcation physics established in P1 (F-1.4: β=1/2 analytic derivation; F-1.5: λ perturbation derivation). Full verification of SFW-1 physics-grounded steps inherits P1 dependency chain. P1 is PHASE-READY on main with one open placeholder (PH-1.1: β=1/2 class-level empirical test, pending P2 C-2.1). PH-1.1 does not block P1 phase-readiness; SFW-1 phase-readiness is not blocked.
placeholder_register.target_session: OPEN — resolves when P1 PH-1.1 closes (β=1/2 class-level empirical confirmation via P2 C-2.1)

software_metadata.git_commit_main: 105c775 (merge f34b7a3 — Merge V12 package into main)
